title: dianthus hirtus subsp. requienii
intro: Dianthus hirtus subsp. requienii, also known as the 'Pennyroyal', is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small, grayish-green leaves and tiny, pink or white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its carminative and antispasmodic effects, which help to ease digestive issues and relieve muscle cramps. The health benefits of Dianthus hirtus subsp. requienii include its ability to improve digestion, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote better sleep. This herb can be prepared in various forms, such as essential oils, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of nausea, headaches, and menstrual cramps.
